授業目的: This course introduces students to the study of Chinese migration to Japan since the mid-19th century. It places this movement in the context of the historical trajectory of modern Japan, the history of the international Chinese diaspora, and the general framework of global human migration.
到達目標: After taking this course student will gain a historically grounded analytical understanding of the movement of Chinese people to Japan in modern times. They will learn to appreciate the economic, political, and social forces behind not just Chinese migration to Japan but human movement within the world system.
特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（中国人移民）（CIEC）/The Chinese in Modern Japan（国際学部）
授業方法: Lectures, fieldwork, discussion, presentations, and writing assignments.

第1回: Read & be prepared to discuss in class
第13回:
[Flex]
Fieldwork & report: Nankinmachi, Kanteibyo Temple, Kobe Chinese museum, Kobe municipal museum
第14回:
[Flex]
Fieldwork & report: Nankinmachi, Kanteibyo Temple, Kobe Chinese museum, Kobe municipal museum
第15回:
[Flex]
Fieldwork: Nankinmachi, Kanteibyo Temple, Kobe Chinese museum, Kobe municipal museum

教科書/Required texts0: All readings are in LUNA or available for free download from open sources.
